growth_records.strain: Gö1 (Mm Mut 203b [empty pWM321 vector control] and Mm Mut 208 [Cas1solo/pRS1270 overexpression], derived from wildtype DSM3647)
growth_records.agit: static (deep-well plates, no shaking during growth)
growth_records.cite: Gehlert et al. Active in vivo translocation of the Methanosarcina mazei Gö1 Casposon. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad474. [PMC10359463]
growth_records.snippet: The long-term evolution experiment ran for 472 days, which corresponds to approximately 600 generations by interpolating the lowest doubling time. The doubling time ranged around 16.5 ± 2.5 h for the different treatments
growth_records.notes: Growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time_h = 0.6931/16.5 = 0.042 h^-1. This 16.5 ± 2.5 h doubling time is an aggregate value stated across the four stress treatments (native/+N, 500 mM NaCl, 0.5 µg/ml mitomycin C, 40°C) tested in the long-term evolution experiment, not resolved per-treatment in the main text (per-treatment values only in Supplementary Table S2, not accessible in this XML). Medium was anaerobic minimal medium (N2:CO2 80:20 headspace) supplemented with methanol (150 mM) and acetate (40 mM) as carbon/energy sources, pre-reduced with Na2S/cysteine/NaOH; base temperature 37°C for native/NaCl/mitomycin treatments (40°C for the temperature-stress treatment only).
